message:
fix(mvcc,ondisk,core): fix TOCTOU race in prune_safe and harden RAID stripe arithmetic

MVCC (ffs-mvcc):
- Fix TOCTOU race in ShardedMvccStore::prune_safe: hold active_snapshots
  write lock while computing watermark AND pruning, preventing snapshot
  registration between watermark read and version pruning

On-disk BTRFS (ffs-ondisk):
- Add checked arithmetic for RAID0/RAID10/RAID5/6 stripe calculations to
  prevent integer overflow when stripe_len * num_stripes exceeds u64 range
- Use checked_mul/checked_add throughout stripe physical offset computation
  in resolve_raid0_stripe, resolve_raid10_stripes, resolve_raid56_stripe

Core (ffs-core):
- Fix readlink unused variable warning (_ino parameter usage)

Beads: updated issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-y363","title":"Convert ignored FUSE e2e tests to runtime-skipped smoke coverage","description":"Replace blanket #[ignore="requires /dev/fuse"] usage in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs with runtime skip guards where feasible, preserving deterministic CI behavior while increasing default coverage signal. Include targeted tests and gate verification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:15:46.869645348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:31:17.043630327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:31:17.043610981Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ycsg","title":"Fix bounds check in MVCC resolve_data_with and checked arithmetic in repair codec","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:57:05.153773060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:57:14.316333208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T01:57:14.315474985Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 bugs: (1) MVCC compression resolve_data_with missing bounds check before chain[index] access - added index >= chain.len() guard returning None; (2) repair codec encode_group unchecked block address addition - replaced with checked_add returning RepairFailed on overflow; (3) repair codec decode_group same unchecked addition in source block reads and recovered block mapping. 745 tests pass (385 ffs-mvcc + 360 ffs-repair).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mvcc","repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-ye4","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement extent mapping (ffs-extent) for regular files","description":"Goal: map file logical offsets to physical blocks using the ext4 extent tree.

Deliverables:
- ffs-extent: a function that takes an inode extent root and returns an iterator of (logical_block -> physical_start,len,unwritten?).
- Support depth>0 extent trees by reading extent blocks from disk (requires BlockDevice).
- Provide a read_extent_at(file_block) helper for file reads.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover: leaf-only extents and index+leaf extents.
- Verified on a fixture file with known extents (compare to debugfs stats or golden outputs).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:46.487585491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:36:21.615868955Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:36:21.615851281Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.350893907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.455657288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-yhro","title":"Fix request-scope API drift in ffs-core","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation after the request-scope API changes in `ffs-core`/`vfs` drifted out of sync.

## Observed failures
- `commit_request_scope` added with invalid visibility / wrong trait placement
- `TxnAbortReason` path no longer resolves
- `fsyncdir` implementation signature no longer matches trait
- `FfsError::Conflict` variant no longer exists

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` moves past the current `ffs-core` request-scope failures
- `ffs-core` trait and impl signatures are consistent
- error mapping uses a valid `FfsError` variant or helper
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:44:04.891190946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:44:04.891190946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","core","vfs"]}
+{"id":"bd-yhro","title":"Fix request-scope API drift in ffs-core","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation after the request-scope API changes in `ffs-core`/`vfs` drifted out of sync.

## Observed failures
- `commit_request_scope` added with invalid visibility / wrong trait placement
- `TxnAbortReason` path no longer resolves
- `fsyncdir` implementation signature no longer matches trait
- `FfsError::Conflict` variant no longer exists

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` moves past the current `ffs-core` request-scope failures
- `ffs-core` trait and impl signatures are consistent
- error mapping uses a valid `FfsError` variant or helper

 {"id":"bd-ynuv","title":"Add E2E FUSE mount integration tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:05:49.393331130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:38.263294368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:38.263267789Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ynv2","title":"Enable default-run FUSE E2E smoke tests with runtime skip gating","description":"Unignore a minimal stable subset of ffs-harness FUSE E2E tests that already self-skip when prerequisites are missing, so capable hosts get coverage by default without failing restricted environments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:46:38.317832135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937747106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937727900Z","close_reason":"Unignored fuse_getattr_file_metadata and fuse_readlink_and_symlink_detection — both are read-only smoke tests that already runtime-skip via fuse_available(). Now 5 tests run by default (read_hello_txt, readdir_root, read_nested_file, getattr_file_metadata, readlink_and_symlink_detection). Write-path and btrfs tests remain gated with #[ignore]. All gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z7n","title":"Track: Foundations (ffs-types + ffs-error)","description":"Define canonical newtypes, parsing primitives, and error model that every other crate depends on.\n\nAcceptance: all normative types (BlockNumber, BlockSize, InodeNumber strategy, TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot, ByteOffset, DeviceId, etc.) exist in ffs-types with documented invariants; ffs-error has stable variant set + errno mapping used by FUSE. All other crates use these types (no local duplicates).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:25.718123153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112602115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112583771Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.228334190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-1ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.370722205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-2oa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.603178334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-3hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.526401085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-owq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.446053435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-sik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.299955368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-z7n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Why this track is first-class: filesystem bugs love unit confusion (bytes vs blocks) and inconsistent error semantics (silent truncation, wrong errno).

North star:
- ffs-types: strong newtypes + checked arithmetic so invariants are enforced at compile time.
- ffs-error: a stable user-facing error set + errno mapping for FUSE.

If this track is sloppy, every other track becomes untestable and unsafe.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:38Z"}]}
